prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for aniline using **DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (tier standard, density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, neutral singlet. The fukui-reactivity task calculated Mulliken partial charges on the neutral, cationic (+1, doublet), and anionic (−1, doublet) species, and derived per-atom Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and the dual descriptor. All convergence and charge-conservation checks passed. The most electrophilic site is the ortho carbon (atom 3, f⁺ = 2.2975867), and the most nucleophilic site is the nitrogen (atom 1, f⁻ = 0.20452359), consistent with aniline's electronic structure. The live log streamed to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/argo_claude-haiku-4.5/20260702-195145_aniline_fukui_reactivity/fukui_20260702-195210.out`.</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">14
